title: inclusion of podman network
body:
adding podman network and the subcommands inspect, list, and rm.  the
inspect subcommand displays the raw cni network configuration.  the list
subcommand displays a summary of the cni networks ala ps.  and the rm
subcommand removes a cni network.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago looks like i am going to need you to walk me through whats going on here ... i didnt expect system tests for a function that didnt exist! cool

i'm assuming you refer to [this system test error log](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/3719/checks?check_run_id=184957524)

The usage messages are bad; they are misleading: `network inspect --help` lists `[flags]` after the mandatory argument. Solution: change the `use` line:
```
-               Use:   "inspect NETWORK",
+               Use:   "inspect [flags] NETWORK",
```
Continue as needed; you will also need to do that with `rm`, and will need to amend the `ls` usage to remove the mandatory `CONTAINER`, i.e. just change it to `ls [flags] [CONTAINER...]`
@edsantiago i have no idea why cobra is doing that ... several of these commands do not have flags.  i did fix up some syntaxes.

I think cobra _always_ add `[flags]`; I remember looking one time and not finding any way to avoid it. (That may have changed since then). Unfortunately we need to waste a lot of effort working around limitations in cobra; fortunately that is mostly one-time work when adding new subcommands. And overall I still think cobra is a pretty good choice for parsing options - thanks for your work in making that happen.
@baude fyi all kinds of test unhappiness

> Will this satisfy #3481

they probably want create which i dont provide here ...
addressed the config comment ... should be ready now
